meta.name: PHQ-4 Patient Health Questionnaire
meta.description: The PHQ-4 is a 4-item ultra-brief screening tool that combines the PHQ-2 (depression) and GAD-2 (anxiety) to provide a quick assessment of both depressive and anxiety symptoms. It is useful as a first-step screening in primary care and general population surveys.
meta.author: Kroenke, Spitzer, Williams & Löwe (2009)
meta.reference: Kroenke K, Spitzer RL, Williams JBW, Löwe B. An ultra-brief screening scale for anxiety and depression: the PHQ-4. Psychosomatics. 2009;50(6):613-621.
meta.instruction: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems?

questions.text: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your total score is within the normal range. No significant psychological distress detected. This indicates that your mood has been relatively stable recently, with no significant signs of persistent sadness or loss of interest.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle
Keep up regular exercise and social activities
Practice relaxation techniques for general well-being
Maintain regular social activities and hobbies
Set aside time each day for activities you enjoy

ranges.label: Mild Distress
ranges.description: You may be experiencing mild psychological distress. Review your subscale scores for more specific information. These mild symptoms may occasionally affect your energy and motivation, but they typically do not interfere significantly with daily functioning.
suggestions: Pay attention to your emotional well-being
Practice stress management techniques
Maintain regular sleep and exercise routines
If symptoms persist, consider consulting a professional
Try 30 minutes of aerobic exercise daily, such as walking or cycling

ranges.label: Moderate Distress
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate psychological distress. Consider completing the full PHQ-9 and/or GAD-7 for more detailed assessment. At this level, feelings of low mood may cause persistent fatigue and reduced interest in activities, potentially affecting daily life and relationships.
suggestions: Consider completing the full PHQ-9 and GAD-7 assessments
Consult a healthcare professional for further evaluation
Practice daily relaxation and stress management
Maintain social connections and support networks
Consider consulting a mental health counselor for professional guidance

ranges.label: Severe Distress
ranges.description: Your score suggests severe psychological distress. Professional evaluation and support are strongly recommended. Your score suggests notable emotional distress that may be significantly affecting your quality of life. Reaching out to a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Seek consultation with a mental health professional promptly
Complete the full PHQ-9 and GAD-7 for detailed assessment
Ensure you have a reliable support system
Do not hesitate to seek help; treatment is effective
Schedule an appointment with a mental health professional as soon as possible

depression.label: Normal
depression.description: Depression subscale score is within the normal range. This indicates that your mood has been relatively stable recently, with no significant signs of persistent sadness or loss of interest.
suggestions: Maintain regular social activities and hobbies
Set aside time each day for activities you enjoy
Practice gratitude by noting positive moments in your life
Keep a consistent sleep schedule and exercise routine
Stay connected with friends and family

depression.label: Possible Depression
depression.description: Score suggests possible depression. Consider completing the full PHQ-9. At this level, feelings of low mood may cause persistent fatigue and reduced interest in activities, potentially affecting daily life and relationships.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health counselor for professional guidance
Try self-help exercises based on cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT)
Wake up at a fixed time each day to establish daily structure
Set small, achievable daily goals to gradually restore activity levels
Reduce alcohol intake and avoid unhealthy coping strategies

anxiety.label: Normal
anxiety.description: Anxiety subscale score is within the normal range. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching
Keep up a regular exercise routine
Cultivate mindful awareness habits
Maintain strong social connections

anxiety.label: Possible Anxiety
anxiety.description: Score suggests possible anxiety. Consider completing the full GAD-7. At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
suggestions: Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is highly effective for anxiety
Practice relaxation techniques daily and maintain regular exercise
Identify and reduce avoidance behaviors; gradually face anxiety-provoking situations
Maintain regular sleep and exercise habits
